turns.content: I'm getting a failure on my CI/CD pipeline because cargo semver-checks reports major version incompatibilities. I know how to use the Json wrapper and serde_json::Value for serialization, but I don't understand why changing an existing field in my crate's public API breaks the build even though I didn't add or remove any fields.

That sounds frustrating when you are trying to fix a failing CI/CD pipeline. The issue is likely that you unintentionally changed an existing field from `Option<T>` to `T` or vice versa, which counts as a breaking change in Semver compatibility rules even without removing items. To avoid this, you should implement a sealed trait strategy for your public types so downstream crates cannot rely on specific implementations. You can use the cargo semver-checks tool to detect these issues early. Have you considered using traits with associated constants to enforce immutability of certain fields?

I think I might have accidentally changed a field type without realizing it, but I want to make sure the downstream crates stay compatible. Can we use sealed trait strategy here? How do I implement that with traits and associated constants?

You can define a trait with an associated constant that restricts which types are allowed, effectively creating a seal for your public API. This way, downstream crates cannot rely on specific implementations without matching the sealed interface. When you run cargo semver-checks, it will verify that your changes do not break compatibility rules defined by these traits. If the build still fails after checking those constants, we should look at how the Json wrapper interacts with your types to ensure no unexpected deserialization issues are causing false positives.
